forecast-period.text: A ridge of high pressure over SA will weaken briefly as a cold front crosses the southeast on Wednesday afternoon. The ridge will begin to re-establish in the wake of the front and stabilise over SA with the strong high pressure centre moving into the Bight over the weekend.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches in the west. Isolated showers developing about the agricultural area and West Coast district, becoming more frequent about the southern agricultural area in the afternoon and evening. Possible small hail with gusty showers in the evening about the far southeast. Maximum temperatures near to below average. South to southeasterly winds, tending northwesterly in the southeast ahead of a fresh and gusty southerly change in the afternoon and evening, strong about the coasts.
Mostly dry, apart from some isolated showers about the southeast. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

Isolated showers about the agricultural areas and West Coast district, scattered near southeastern and far western coasts. Dry elsewhere. Possible small hail in the morning about the far southeast. Maximum temperatures below average. Fresh southwest to southeasterly winds, gusty in the southeast.
Isolated showers about the southern agricultural area. Dry in the north. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, fresh about the coasts.

text.p: Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area. Dry in the north. Maximum temperatures near to below average. South to southeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Saturday: Less than 2 mm across the northern agricultural areas and southern pastoral districts, increasing to 2 to 5mm across the southern agricultural area. Higher falls of 5 to 10mm are possible across southwards facing coasts and up to 15mm in the Mount Lofty Ranges and the far southeast.
